content.text: Learning captured: Inserted phase 6 (Stabilize spawn-tree parsing and JSON output): Fix all 3 spawn-tree blockers: (1) Fix O(n^2) parse_spawn_tree scaling that causes test timeouts, (2) JSON-escape ant names before interpolation into JSON output strings, (3) Add structural JSON validation in spawn-tree-load before passing through json_ok
Learning captured: When adding awk gsub escaping, order matters: backslash must be first to prevent double-escaping
Learning captured: Literal newlines break awk NF guards, CR is the testable control char for embedded content
Phase 1 (Harden JSON escaping) completed. Key patterns: gsub order matters (backslash first), CR is primary testable control char for pipe-delimited records
Learning captured: awk -v interprets C-style escapes — use ENVIRON[] for user content
Learning captured: Multi-line strings cannot pass via awk -v — use head/tail concatenation
Phase 2 (Fix queen-promote newline destruction) completed. Key patterns: use ENVIRON[] not awk -v for user content, head/tail for multi-line block replacement
Phase 3 (Filter test noise) completed. Guard pattern: AETHER_TESTING=1 check before activity.log writes
Phase 4 (Update high-CVE deps) completed. npm overrides for transitive dep pinning, 0 vulnerabilities
Phase 5 (Final verification) completed. All 5 phases done: spawn-tree JSON escaping, queen-promote awk fix, test noise filter, CVE deps, midden cleanup
[decision] test decision
[decision] test "quoted" decision
[decision] test with "quotes"
Phase 1 (context-update json_ok) completed. 10/11 already safe from prior colony, 1 fixed.
Learning captured: Empty data files (0 bytes) cause jq to produce no output — always guard with -s check before jq on data files
Learning captured: Shell 'local' keyword only valid inside functions — never use in case statements at script top level
Phase 2 (Harden commit-message + changelog + activity-log json_ok sites) completed. Key patterns: empty data files need -s guard before jq; local keyword invalid outside functions
Phase 3 (Harden remaining scattered json_ok sites) completed. Key patterns: Builder agents may report success without modifying files; independent Watcher verification catches fabricated completions
Learning captured: Builder agents may report success without actually modifying files — independent Watcher verification is essential
Phase 2 (Harden commit-message + changelog + activity-log json_ok sites) completed. All 5 json_ok sites converted to jq --arg safe escaping. Empty-file guard added to validate-state.

Learning captured: json_ok hardening has two distinct injection classes: string interpolation in JSON output (json_ok pattern) and variable interpolation in jq filter expressions (.${var} pattern) — both require --arg parameterization but at different layers
Learning captured: Quality gate re-audit after fixing HIGH findings raised score from 53 to 73, confirming the fixes addressed the root issues not just symptoms

Phase 3 (Harden remaining scattered json_ok sites) completed. Key patterns: two distinct injection classes identified (json_ok string interpolation + jq filter expression injection); Auditor quality gate drove discovery of view-state vulnerabilities
Learning captured: Archaeology pre-build scans prevent regression when modifying same lines as prior bug fixes
Learning captured: After monolith extraction tests that grep source code break when code moves to extracted modules

Phase 5 (Fix hive-read null safety and learning recovery tests) completed. Key patterns: archaeology pre-build scans catch prior fixes at exact change sites; stale grep targets after extraction need module-level updates
Phase 1 (State Coordination Design) completed. Key patterns: branch-local vs hub-global state classification; pheromone snapshot/merge-back protocol; idempotent midden collection
CI/CD pipeline active -- ensure changes pass all checks
Testing infrastructure present (64 test files) -- maintain TDD discipline
Large codebase -- scope changes carefully, prefer incremental work
Full PR workflow -- implement worktree isolation, task-as-PR, 5-tier review pipeline, quality gates as CI checks, sequential merging, and cross-branch state coordination
Each colony task should create its own git branch and PR -- agents never work directly on the main branch
sync-parity: Claude/OpenCode command and agent consistency
docs-accuracy: documentation matches reality on disk
utils-integrity: aether-utils.sh dispatcher and module consistency
cross-refs: all file paths and references resolve
packaging: npm packaging and distribution correctness
[error-pattern] Category "quality" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
[error-pattern] Category "resilience" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
[error-pattern] Category "documentation" recurring (3+ occurrences in midden)
Avoid modifying files directly on the main branch during builds -- all changes go through PRs
Phase 1 (Go Module Init & Storage Foundation) completed. Key patterns: parallel builders for independent packages, atomic writes via temp+rename, per-path mutexes for concurrent safety
test signal
avoid this
nice work
Phase 2 (Port Remaining Shell Commands to Go) completed. All 141 shell subcommands now have Go equivalents.
Phase 2 (File Locking) completed. Key patterns: syscall.Flock works for cross-process safety; single Mutex bottleneck under high contention; lock upgrade/downgrade is non-atomic
Phase 3 (Fix generate-commit-message seal/milestone types) completed. Added seal/milestone/pause/contextual to validCommitTypes. Key: keep validation slice and help string in sync.
Phase 4 (Fix learning-check-promotion --all and learning-promote-auto stub) completed. Key patterns: connect stubs to real service methods, use make([]T,0) not nil slices for JSON consistency
Phase 2 (pheromone dedup) completed. Key patterns: content-hash dedup with reinforcement counting prevents duplicate signals
Phase 3 (Fix state-mutate bracket notation) completed. Key patterns: regex widening + normalizeBracketPath is cleaner than teaching sjson bracket paths
Canvas deposit at (258, 57)
Canvas deposit at (130, 343)
Update and install pipeline — all sync paths must work correctly
Pheromone system — verify end-to-end pipeline integrity
Dont skip existing tests — all 1075+ must continue passing
Phase 6 (Fix Learning and Memory Pipeline Calls) completed. Key pattern: broad markdown sweeps in phases 2-5 already fixed learning pipeline calls — no additional fixes needed.
Phase 7 (Fix Flag System Calls) completed. Key: positional arg fixes often reveal deeper bugs — check persistence and struct fields too.
Phase 8 (Registry + Context-Update Calls) completed. Key: flag aliases in Go CLI are cleaner than changing all markdown — backward compatible with priority.
Phase 9 (Fix Instinct-Create and Remaining Calls) completed. Key pattern: positional arg sweeps need per-command grep, not single-pass file scan.
Phase 10 (Comprehensive Verification) completed. 89 broken CLI calls remain in .claude/ and .opencode/ dirs (not covered by prior sweeps). All 1207 Go tests pass.
Phase 11 (Final Cleanup and Documentation) completed. Key patterns: parallel cleanup tasks work independently; pheromone noise cleanup keeps signal-to-noise healthy

pattern.content: test_pattern
test_uat_pattern
converting bare exec.Command to exec.CommandContext
timeout error handling in command context functions
implementing file locking in Go
replacing bare context.Background() with cobra cmd.Context()-derived contexts
adding timeout configuration to cobra commands
building on a well-designed abstraction from a prior phase
running final integration phase after sequential fixes
setting nested JSON fields with sjson
exporting colony archive XML
Cobra alias command shares RunE with nested subcommand
a stub command that counts but never performs its action
syscall.Flock provides clean cross-process locking but a single in-process Mutex serializes all paths — use per-path mutex for better concurrency under high contention
Lock upgrade/downgrade (shared to exclusive or vice versa) is silently destructive with flock — the unlock-reacquire window breaks atomicity
When extending conventional commit type allowlists in Cobra commands, update both the validation slice and the --flag help string to prevent drift — use a derived help string from the slice

When implementing auto-promotion pipelines, connect stub implementations to actual service methods (e.g., PromoteService.Promote()) rather than leaving them as counting-only operations — callers expect side effects, not just counts

When adding batch mode flags to existing Cobra commands (e.g., --all), ensure the flag name doesn't conflict with existing positional args and that the output schema is consistent between single-item and batch modes
building Cobra CLI commands that need both single-ID and batch modes
a Go command stub returns placeholder counts without calling the real service
migrating shell commands to Go binary
Running final verification on Go projects
Adding new CLI flags to Go commands
cobra command rejects positional args via NoArgs but callers pass positional JSON
writing duplicate signals to pheromone store
state-mutate needs to accept bracket notation paths
deprecating dead CLI commands
silent error sites in non-critical code paths
updating agent definition markdown files that reference shell utilities
batch-replacing references in documentation files that mix prose and command invocations
deprecating dead shell scripts after Go migration
benchmarking Go CLI commands
When centralizing validation logic across multiple call sites
When building a generic cache for arbitrary JSON data
deduplicating file loads across commands
function spawns external process (git, curl, etc) and is called multiple times
cobra.MaximumNArgs(N) with positional arg + flag mutual exclusion is a clean backward-compat pattern for CLI commands
Content-hash based dedup with reinforcement counting is a clean pattern for idempotent signal writes
Widening regex to accept bracket notation then normalizing to dot notation is cleaner than teaching sjson bracket paths
The deprecated command stub pattern provides a clean migration path that preserves CLI surface area while removing implementation
Adding log.Printf to previously-silent error sites is zero-risk observability — errors remain non-fatal but failures become visible in logs
when implementing protected directory/file patterns
when builders spawn worktrees during tests
Testing pheromone or signal deduplication logic
Adding new context sections to colony-prime or pr-context
adding a new string enum type to ColonyState
When wiring conditional behavior into build playbooks based on colony configuration
injecting colony context fields into worker prompts
adding display fields to status or resume commands
When replacing dead or deprecated command blocks in playbooks
documentation phase for new feature
adding new CLI flags to cobra commands
activity-log positional args silently ignored — all 48 markdown files converted to --command/--details flags with source+details merged into --details field
memory-capture, learning-promote-auto, trust-score-compute, and event-bus-publish calls were already fixed during phases 2-5 broad sweeps
fixing CLI flag commands that silently ignore args
fixing CLI commands that markdown calls with different flag names
Running broad grep sweeps to fix CLI calls
activity-log positional args silently ignored — all 48 markdown files converted to --command/--details flags with source+details merged into --details field
memory-capture, learning-promote-auto, trust-score-compute, and event-bus-publish calls were already fixed during phases 2-5 broad sweeps

flag-add positional args fixed by adding --description/--phase flags to Go CLI and converting 13 markdown calls; flag-resolve and flag-acknowledge had real bugs (missing persistence, missing struct fields) beyond just positional args

registry-add needed flag aliases (--path for --repo, --tags for --domain) plus new --goal flag and positional version support. context-update needed 4 new sub-actions and --section/--key/--content flags. Both required Go CLI changes, not just markdown fixes.
Positional arg sweeps need per-command grep across ALL files - single-pass sweeps miss calls in unvisited files
Phase 1-9 broad sweeps fixed .aether/docs/command-playbooks/ files but missed .claude/commands/ant/ and .opencode/commands/ant/ — per-directory systematic grep is needed
Colony cleanup phases benefit from parallel builders: worktree cleanup, docs update, and data verification can run independently with zero conflicts
Pheromone data accumulates noise from test signals and imports — regular cleanup (removing empty content, expired signals) keeps the signal-to-noise ratio healthy
